name: Tension Pneumothorax Deterioration
description: Post-intubation ICU patient who develops tension pneumothorax. Progressive deterioration from stable baseline to cardiovascular collapse over ~7 minutes unless needle decompression is performed.

action.text: 42-year-old female, intubated and mechanically ventilated following motor vehicle collision. Bilateral breath sounds present. Hemodynamically stable on minimal sedation.

timeline.label: Early signs — rising peak airway pressures

action.text: Ventilator alarming for high peak pressures. Patient appears mildly agitated.

timeline.label: Tension developing — compensatory tachycardia, early desaturation

action.description: Right-sided tension pneumothorax developing. Mediastinal shift beginning. Absent breath sounds on right.

action.text: Without needle decompression, the patient progresses to pulseless electrical activity and cardiac arrest.
